body: [{"ac":1,"text":"ddx work --watch never holds .git/index.lock across an LLM subprocess (claude/codex/gemini/etc.) invocation; lock is released before the subprocess starts and re-acquired only for the actual git mutation. Verified by an integration test that runs an execute-bead and asserts .git/index.lock mtime is older than the subprocess invocation timeline.","kind":"negative","verifiable":true},{"ac":2,"text":"Same property for .ddx/.git-tracker.lock: scope to the actual tracker file write, not across subprocess wait.","kind":"prose","verifiable":false},{"ac":3,"text":"Lock lifetime metric is emitted as a structured event (JSON line with lock_name, acquired_at, released_at, duration_ms, holder_pid, operation) for every acquire/release; visible in ddx logs.","kind":"prose","verifiable":false},{"ac":4,"text":"A configurable hold-time cap (default 10s for index.lock, 30s for tracker lock) is enforced: if a holder exceeds the cap, the worker logs an error, releases, and surfaces the violation in its execution evidence.","kind":"prose","verifiable":false},{"ac":5,"text":"A multi-worker integration test (5+ concurrent ddx work --watch on independent beads) asserts p99 lock hold time stays under the cap and no operator-issued ddx bead create/update/close in a sibling shell times out.","kind":"prose","verifiable":false},{"ac":6,"text":"Documentation update in ddx README or AGENTS.md naming the lock-lifetime contract.","kind":"mechanical","verifiable":true}]

{"ac_map":[{"parent_ac":"1. ddx work --watch never holds .git/index.lock across an LLM subprocess (claude/codex/gemini/etc.) invocation; lock is released before the subprocess starts and re-acquired only for the actual git mutation. Verified by an integration test that runs an execute-bead and asserts .git/index.lock mtime is older than the subprocess invocation timeline.","coverage":"scope-locks-to-mutation-window AC1 (TestExecuteBead_IndexLockNotHeldAcrossSubprocess) + AC3 (TestExecuteBead_LocksReacquiredForMutation)"},{"parent_ac":"2. Same property for .ddx/.git-tracker.lock: scope to the actual tracker file write, not across subprocess wait.","coverage":"scope-locks-to-mutation-window AC2 (TestExecuteBead_TrackerLockNotHeldAcrossSubprocess)"},{"parent_ac":"3. Lock lifetime metric is emitted as a structured event (JSON line with lock_name, acquired_at, released_at, duration_ms, holder_pid, operation) for every acquire/release; visible in ddx logs.","coverage":"lock-lifetime-metric AC1 (TestLockMetrics_EmitsAcquireAndReleaseEvents) + AC2 (TestLockMetrics_DurationMatchesElapsed) + AC3 (TestLockMetrics_VisibleInDdxLogs)"},{"parent_ac":"4. A configurable hold-time cap (default 10s for index.lock, 30s for tracker lock) is enforced: if a holder exceeds the cap, the worker logs an error, releases, and surfaces the violation in its execution evidence.","coverage":"lock-hold-time-cap AC1 (TestLockCap_DefaultIndexLockCap10s) + AC2 (TestLockCap_ExceedingCapForceReleases) + AC3 (TestLockCap_ViolationWrittenToEvidence) + AC4 (TestLockCap_ViolationLoggedAsError)"},{"parent_ac":"5. A multi-worker integration test (5+ concurrent ddx work --watch on independent beads) asserts p99 lock hold time stays under the cap and no operator-issued ddx bead create/update/close in a sibling shell times out.","coverage":"multi-worker-lock-contention-test AC1-AC4 (TestIntegration_MultiWorkerLockContention_5Workers / _P99IndexLockUnderCap / _P99TrackerLockUnderCap / _NoOperatorTimeouts)"},{"parent_ac":"6. Documentation update in ddx README or AGENTS.md naming the lock-lifetime contract.","coverage":"document-lock-lifetime-contract AC1 + AC2 (TestDocs_LockContractSectionPresent)"}],"child_ids":["ddx-329a08b8","ddx-68f5623c","ddx-bfd6d9ae","ddx-6650fdc9","ddx-f0a70134"],"rationale":"Split along the natural seams of the parent: (1) the behavioral fix (lock scoping) is a self-contained refactor that the integration test in (4) ultimately validates; (2) instrumentation is a precondition for (3) cap enforcement, which reuses the same wrapper; (4) the concurrency test depends on (1) being in place and (2) for its metric assertions; (5) docs are trivially independent. Each child is small enough to execute in a single bead and has a clear test surface. Dependencies: cap (3) -\u003e metric (2); concurrency test (4) -\u003e scoping fix (1) + metric (2); docs (5) -\u003e cap (3)."}

body:
verification_command=cd cli && go test ./internal/lockmetrics/... ./internal/integration/...
exit_code=0
output:
ok  	github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/lockmetrics	0.659s
ok  	github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/integration	10.873s

verification_command passed: cd cli && go test ./internal/lockmetrics/... ./internal/integration/...
rationale: verification_command: cd cli && go test ./internal/lockmetrics/... ./internal/integration/...

This is an umbrella/parent bead. Its six acceptance criteria were delivered by
its five decomposed children, all now closed and merged onto the base revision
(98bf55b012ef142fc643b1dd85c1acfc55f1dbc9):

- ddx-329a08b8 (closed) — scope .git/index.lock and .ddx/.git-tracker.lock to
  the git/tracker mutation window, never across the LLM subprocess.
  Satisfies parent AC1 + AC2.
- ddx-68f5623c (closed) — structured acquire/release events
  (lock_name, operation, holder_pid, acquired_at, released_at, duration_ms) in
  cli/internal/lockmetrics/lockmetrics.go (Event struct).
  Satisfies parent AC3.
- ddx-bfd6d9ae (closed) — configurable hold-time cap (DefaultIndexLockCap=10s,
  DefaultTrackerLockCap=30s; overridable via DDX_LOCK_CAP_INDEX_MS /
  DDX_LOCK_CAP_TRACKER_MS) with fail-fast force-release and lock-violation.json
  evidence in cli/internal/lockmetrics/lockcap.go.
  Satisfies parent AC4.
- ddx-6650fdc9 (closed) — multi-worker integration test
  (cli/internal/integration/lock_contention_test.go:
  TestIntegration_MultiWorkerLockContention_5Workers, _P99IndexLockUnderCap,
  _P99TrackerLockUnderCap, _NoOperatorTimeouts) proving 5 concurrent
  `ddx work --watch` workers keep p99 hold times under cap with no operator
  bead create/update/close timing out.
  Satisfies parent AC5.
- ddx-f0a70134 (closed) — "Lock Lifetime Contract" section in AGENTS.md, asserted
  by TestDocs_LockContractSectionPresent.
  Satisfies parent AC6.

No source change is owed by the parent itself; the verification_command above
re-runs the lockmetrics unit tests (AC3, AC4) and the integration suite (AC1,
AC2, AC5, and the AC6 doc-presence test). Both packages passed locally
(lockmetrics 0.68s, integration 11.3s). Exit 0 confirms all six ACs hold and
the parent may close.
result_rev=98bf55b012ef142fc643b1dd85c1acfc55f1dbc9
base_rev=98bf55b012ef142fc643b1dd85c1acfc55f1dbc9
outcome_reason=no_changes
